title: Aldama gardneri
intro: Aldama gardneri, also known as Gardner's Aldama, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to alleviate inflammation, reduce fever, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Aldama gardneri include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Aldama gardneri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is a member of the genus Aldama, which is a group of plants native to the Americas. The plant is also known as a member of the family Thymelaeaceae, but some sources classify it as a member of Malvaceae. Aldama gardneri is often included in the family Thymelaeaceae by some sources. The classification of Aldama gardneri can vary depending on the source and the current taxonomic understanding.
